destinations.evidence: Wallet verification propagates delivery config decode failures as errors for every family it supports with callback cost, rather than substituting zero.
Unhandled precompiles fail wallet validation before any zero callback-cost acceptance path can make them pass.
destinations.snippet: return Err(format!("unsupported precompile for wallet verification: {precompile_address}"));

equivalence_argument: For wallet-supported two-phase families, malformed delivery config aborts validation; unsupported precompiles also fail instead of being accepted with zero callback cost.
notes: This mapping is scoped to the precompile families accepted by verify_wallet_for_commitment; broader all-family callback coverage is recorded separately as invariant 539.
rationale: The primary diff removes the previous decode-failure branches that logged a warning and returned `U256::ZERO`, replacing them with `map_err(...)?` and an explicit error for unhandled two-phase precompiles. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If wallet escrow verification cannot decode the delivery config for a supported two-phase precompile, it must return an error instead of using zero callback cost; unhandled two-phase precompiles must also error.
suggested_falsification_tests: Pass malformed delivery-config calldata for long-running HTTP, ZK, FHE, sovereign agent, and persistent agent precompiles to wallet verification and assert it returns an error rather than accepting with zero Phase 2 callback cost.
summary: Malformed delivery config rejects escrow validation.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Pass malformed delivery-config calldata for long-running HTTP, audio, ZK, sovereign agent, and persistent agent wallet checks and assert each returns an error.
title: Malformed delivery config rejects escrow validation
